idea.hook: Our Sun: A COSMIC GIANT compared to most stars?!
facts: Our Sun is a G-type star, with a diameter of 1.4 million km (870,000 miles).
Most stars in our Milky Way are Red Dwarfs, significantly smaller and dimmer.
A common Red Dwarf, like TRAPPIST-1, is only about 11% the size of our Sun. You could fit over 8 TRAPPIST-1 stars across the Sun's diameter!
idea.payoff: The 'average' star is much smaller than you imagine – our Sun is surprisingly substantial!
idea.title: Sun: A Cosmic GIANT?! ☀️🤏

scenes.visual: A majestic, bright yellow-orange Sun emerges from a dark, starry background.
A close-up of the Sun, with an animated measurement arc appearing across its diameter, highlighting the value.

scenes.text: Most stars are Red Dwarfs. Smaller & dimmer.
scenes.visual: The screen transitions from the large Sun to a vast field of countless small, dim red stars against a darker, dense starfield.

scenes.text: TRAPPIST-1: 11% the Sun's size. Over 8 fit across Sun!
scenes.visual: A large Sun appears on the left, and 8-9 small red dwarf representations (like TRAPPIST-1) are visually laid out across its diameter on the right.

scenes.text: The 'average' star is TINY. Our Sun is surprisingly substantial!
scenes.visual: A vibrant, glowing Sun is positioned centrally, with a few much smaller red dwarfs orbiting it or scattered in the background, reinforcing its dominance.

script.thumbnail_text: Sun vs. Red Dwarf: Size Surprises!
